title: Hierarchical Memory Address Structure
description: A visual breakdown of how a memory address is structured to access a specific location within a memory module, showing the relationship between the address bus, memory chip, and the targeted byte.
relationships: The CPU places a binary address on the Address Bus
The address bits are split; the most significant bits (MSB) select the row via the Row Decoder
The least significant bits (LSB) select the column via the Column Decoder
The intersection of the selected row and column activates a specific memory cell
The data value (byte) from the activated cell is placed onto the Data Bus back to the CPU
